task_completion.description: Verify that the agent has successfully logged into Rocket.Chat.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat main workspace interface is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent has navigated to the #release-updates channel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The chat header or active highlighted channel in the sidebar clearly shows '#release-updates'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent has opened the dialog to create a new snippet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Create Snippet' or similar file creation modal/dialog is open over the chat interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the snippet is named 'rollback_procedure.sh'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Name' or 'File Name' input field in the snippet dialog contains exactly 'rollback_procedure.sh'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct bash script content is entered into the snippet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text area of the snippet dialog contains the required bash script, starting with '#!/bin/bash' and including the docker service update rollback commands.

task_completion.description: Verify that the snippet was successfully sent to the channel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The snippet 'rollback_procedure.sh' appears as a posted message/attachment in the #release-updates chat history.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Rocket.Chat graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Rocket.Chat web or desktop application UI, not terminal windows executing API/curl commands.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the snippet feature rather than sending a standard chat message.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final message should be formatted as a Rocket.Chat snippet/file attachment, not just a regular chat message containing markdown code blocks.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual Rocket.Chat application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script content should be typed or pasted into the Rocket.Chat interface, not just written in a standalone text editor like Notepad or VSCode.
